questions.stem: These paired breast sections show tumor cells infiltrating in a single-file pattern (left, H&E) and absence of the membranous brown immunostain seen normally (right). The loss of the marker highlighted here most directly impairs which cellular function?

explanation.summary: The single-file infiltration and loss of membranous staining reflect loss of E-cadherin, a homophilic adhesion molecule; its loss disrupts cell-to-cell adhesion, a hallmark of invasive lobular carcinoma. The other functions are unrelated to E-cadherin.
explanation.whyCorrect: The single-file infiltration and loss of membranous staining reflect loss of E-cadherin, a homophilic adhesion molecule; its loss disrupts cell-to-cell adhesion, a hallmark of invasive lobular carcinoma. The other functions are unrelated to E-cadherin.

questions.stem: This uterine sarcoma shows a rounded nest of neoplastic cells filling and expanding a vascular space. A tumor demonstrating this pattern of spread would be expected to metastasize primarily by which route?

explanation.summary: Sarcomas of mesenchymal origin invade vessels and characteristically metastasize hematogenously, as shown by tumor filling the vascular lumen. Lymphatic spread is typical of carcinomas, transcoelomic seeding occurs across body cavities, and perineural/direct extension are different routes.
explanation.whyCorrect: Sarcomas of mesenchymal origin invade vessels and characteristically metastasize hematogenously, as shown by tumor filling the vascular lumen. Lymphatic spread is typical of carcinomas, transcoelomic seeding occurs across body cavities, and perineural/direct extension are different routes.

questions.stem: This gross specimen shows the omentum diffusely studded and thickened by nodular tumor deposits. By which mechanism of spread did the ovarian carcinoma most likely reach this site?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Transcoelomic (seeding) spread across the peritoneal cavity

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hematogenous spread through the portal vein

choices.label: D
choices.text: Direct invasion through the uterine wall

explanation.summary: Ovarian carcinoma classically seeds the peritoneal cavity, coating the omentum with tumor (omental caking) via transcoelomic spread. The other routes do not explain diffuse serosal/omental studding.
explanation.whyCorrect: Ovarian carcinoma classically seeds the peritoneal cavity, coating the omentum with tumor (omental caking) via transcoelomic spread. The other routes do not explain diffuse serosal/omental studding.
